title: Copy, Replace, Rename, Delete, Batch, and Concurrency · Concurrent Code Workshop
summary: A delivery team incrementally builds conditional object-mutation protocol, changes one file or failure, and keeps earlier HTTP, Go, accessibility, security, and resource-budget invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of conditional object-mutation protocol while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation.
Implement rename as version-aware copy, validation, metadata update, reference switch, and safe source deletion with recoverable intermediate states.
Use validators, version IDs, idempotency records, or application locks to detect stale concurrent mutation and expose 409 or 412 meaning.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Copy Replace Metadata
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go file-delivery team handles case 6584 during a cost-anomaly investigation. Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Copy Replace Metadata.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for copy or replace with explicit source version, conditional evidence, metadata directive, encryption, checksum, tags, and destination validation. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Copy Replace Metadata, keep Copy Replace Metadata connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

Build deterministic Go evidence to model a non-atomic version-aware copy, validate, and delete rename protocol; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then cancel halfway through and prove every body, part, and goroutine reaches a terminal state. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Batch Partial Failure
steps.instruction:
